arts.ttl: The unwanted record Hull City must avoid against Luton Town in crunch Championship six-pointer
arts.sum: Boss Ruben Selles will be desperate to halt that alarming run, especially as it comes against the Hatters, who boast the best record in the Championship over the past 15 international breaks dating back to the 2021/22 campaign, but sit second bottom of the table and four points adrift of safety. Their last victory straight off the back of an international break came in November 2021 when the Tigers beat Birmingham City 2-0 in front of 12,409 at the MKM Stadium, thanks to a goal in each half from George Honeyman and Mallik Wilks - City went on to win four straight games during that run under Grant McCann just prior to Acun Ilicali's takeover.

arts.sum: When people watch my team, I want them to see they love to play football. It’s not arrogance, but we are four points away from third with eight games to play.

arts.ttl: Hull City boss offers fresh Louie Barry update as forgotten man closes in on possible return
arts.sum: Now it's 35, 40 days, I think it's around that (until the end of the season), so the message was more in that sense of 30, 35 days, just be sure that you are ready for anything that comes, but our mentality is always to get the game in front of us. The only reason why I refer to those eight games or five weeks is because when we finished the transfer window, I had a talk with the players about it being 87 days or 90 days until the end of the season, so I want everyone to be part of the team.

arts.ttl: Hull City receive timely injury boost before monster Luton Town clash
arts.sum: That's why when you have periods like this international break, you need to be sure that you have a good balance between giving some time off but also keeping the team in a very competitive way. We did that refreshment in the first part of the international break, the first week with a couple of good training sessions and then some time off.

arts.ttl: Hull City player praise fans but bemoans not signing for Manchester City
arts.sum: Hull City attacker Abdülkadir Ömür has told the Turkish media that he still thinks about his opportunity to sign for Manchester City. The Premier League champions were looking to sign the Turkey international earlier in his career, but Trabzonspor, his club at the time, wouldn’t sanction the transfer.
